7. Idukki Dam is on the Periyar River in Kerala.

8. Best Revision Strategy
For competitive exams, don’t try to memorise 100+ dams randomly. Use this order:
Priority 1: Top 30 dams table
Priority 2: River-wise groups: Narmada, Krishna, Kaveri, Chenab, Chambal, Damodar
Priority 3: Superlatives: highest, longest, famous arch dam, largest reservoir
Priority 4: News dams: Subansiri, Ratle, Pakal Dul, Sawalkote, Mullaperiyar, Polavaram,
Farakka
Priority 5: Map practice: locate river + state
For one-day revision, focus only on Dam → River → State and the news-linked dams.

topic: Organs vitamins & diseases
1. Vitamin A – eyes/vision; deficiency: night blindness.
2. Vitamin B1 – nerves; deficiency: beriberi.
3. Vitamin B2 – skin/mouth; deficiency: cheilosis, mouth cracks.
4. Vitamin B3 – skin/nerves; deficiency: pellagra.
5. Vitamin B9 – blood/RBC formation; deficiency: anaemia.
6. Vitamin B12 – nerves + RBC; deficiency: pernicious anaemia.
7. Vitamin C – gums/wound healing; deficiency: scurvy.
8. Vitamin D – bones/calcium absorption; deficiency: rickets/osteomalacia.
9. Vitamin E – reproductive health/antioxidant; deficiency: sterility rare.
10. Vitamin K – blood clotting; deficiency: excess bleeding.
11. Iodine – thyroid gland; deficiency: goitre.
12. Iron – haemoglobin; deficiency: anaemia.
13. Calcium – bones/teeth; deficiency: weak bones, osteoporosis.
14. Liver – produces bile; disease: jaundice, hepatitis.
15. Kidney – filters blood, forms urine; disease: nephritis, kidney stone.
16. Heart – pumps blood; disease: heart attack, hypertension.
17. Lungs – respiration; disease: TB, pneumonia, asthma.
18. Pancreas – insulin secretion; disease: diabetes mellitus.
19. Brain – control centre; disease: stroke, meningitis, epilepsy.
20. Stomach/intestine – digestion/absorption; disease: ulcer, diarrhoea, cholera, typhoid.

I checked the current SSC pattern/trend sources. One important correction: I won’t label
any single question as truly “90% guaranteed,” because SSC randomizes shifts; I’ll mark
them as 90+ revision-priority concepts based on repeatability + syllabus fit + multi-exam
overlap.
SSC GK/GS Deep-Dive: CGL / CHSL / Selection Post Phase
14
Scope: 2021–2026 trend only, with 2026 treated as notification + expected pattern, not
completed PYQ data. CGL 2026 and Selection Post Phase 14 both keep General Awareness
at 25 questions / 50 marks; Selection Post Phase 14 also has 25 GA questions with
negative marking, and its GK syllabus includes current affairs, awards, books, sports,
scientific research, static GK, portfolios, and persons in news.
Oliveboard Testbook
Important correction: no honest mentor can say “this exact question has 90% probability.”
What we can say is: these concepts have 90+ revision priority because they repeatedly fit
SSC’s PYQ style, syllabus, one-liner nature, and cross-exam overlap. SSC CGL/CHSL GA
repeatedly clusters around Science, Polity, History, Current Affairs, Static GK, Geography,
and Economy; CGL sources put current affairs around 4–5 questions, Science 4–5 or higher,
1/77

Polity/History around 3–4 each, while CHSL trend also gives high weight to History,
Science, Polity, Static GK, Geography, and Economics.
Oliveboard Testbook Oliveboard
How SSC asks GK/GS
SSC usually asks direct fact-based questions, not UPSC-style analysis: “Which Article?”,
“Who wrote?”, “Which vitamin?”, “Which river?”, “Which founder?”, “Which state?”, “Which
dance?”, “Which disease-agent?”, “Which amendment?” In older CGL guidance, the GA
section is 25 questions and is high-scoring because it is fast if facts are revised; the listed
static clusters include History, Geography, Economy, Polity, Biology, Chemistry, Physics,
Current Affairs, and Miscellaneous GK.
Jagranjosh.com
Your priority order for CGL/CHSL/Phase 14 GK should be:
Science + Polity + History = core marks
Current Affairs + Static GK = swing marks
Geography + Economy = low-volume but easy-return marks

For SSC CGL and Phase 14, Polity questions are usually asked in these forms:

Amendment → Change
Example: 42nd Amendment = Fundamental Duties + Socialist/Secular/Integrity
Office → Term / retirement age
Example: Supreme Court judge = 65 years

PART 12 — SSC CORRELATION MAP
How SSC Links Topics
1. Article Linkage
SSC loves direct pairings:
Article 14 → Equality before law
Article 17 → Untouchability abolished
Article 21 → Life and personal liberty
Article 32 → Constitutional remedies
73/77

Article 40 → Panchayats
Article 44 → Uniform Civil Code
Article 51A → Fundamental Duties
Article 110 → Money Bill
Article 148 → CAG
Article 324 → Election Commission
2. History Linkage
Repeat chains:
1885 → INC → Bombay → W.C. Bonnerjee
1905 → Bengal Partition → Swadeshi → Curzon
1906 → Muslim League → Dhaka
1907 → Surat Split
1916 → Lucknow Pact
1917 → Champaran → Indigo
1919 → Jallianwala Bagh
1920 → Non-Cooperation
1930 → Dandi March
1942 → Quit India
3. Geography Linkage
SSC asks:
River → origin
River → dam
Lake → state
Pass → state
Soil → crop
National park → state
Crop → season
Mineral → state
Mountain → range
Census → highest/lowest
4. Science Linkage
SSC asks:
Vitamin → deficiency
Disease → pathogen
Organ → function
SI unit → quantity
Instrument → use
74/77

Formula → compound
Alloy → metals
Gas → use
Lens/mirror → application
Law → scientist
5. Static GK Linkage
SSC asks:
Book → author
Award → field
Trophy → sport
Dance → state
Festival → state
Temple → state
Day → date
Organization → HQ
First person → field
National symbol → name
PART 13 — FINAL HIGH-ROI SSC GK/GS REVISION SYSTEM
A. Your Daily GK Slot
Do this daily:
45 minutes Static GK
30 minutes Science
30 minutes Polity/History
20 minutes Geography/Economy
20 minutes Current Affairs
Total: about 2.5 hours.
B. 7-Day Rotation
Day 1: Polity + Biology
Day 2: Modern History + Physics
Day 3: Ancient/Medieval + Chemistry
Day 4: Geography + Static GK
Day 5: Economy + Current Affairs
Day 6: Mixed PYQ revision
Day 7: Full 25-question GA mock + error notebook
75/77

C. Error Notebook Format
Make 4 columns:
Wrong Question Correct Answer Link Fact Next Revision
Article 324 Election Article 148 = CAG 2 days
Commission
D. 90+ Priority Zones
For SSC CGL + Phase 14, these are your strongest zones:
1. Polity articles and amendments
2. Modern history movements
3. INC sessions
4. Governor-General/Viceroy events
5. Vitamins and diseases
6. Biology organs and hormones
7. Physics SI units and instruments
8. Chemistry formulas and alloys
9. Rivers, dams, lakes, passes
10. National parks and wildlife sanctuaries
11. Books and authors
12. Awards and trophies
13. Dance/festival-state mapping
14. Government schemes
15. Last 6–8 months current affairs
E. How to Convert This Into Marks
For every micro-topic:
Round 1: Read all 10 Q&A.
Round 2: Hide answer and recall.
Round 3: Reverse the question.
Example: “Baking soda = NaHCO₃” becomes “NaHCO₃ = Baking soda.”
Round 4: Make one trap fact.
Example: Washing soda = Na₂CO₃·10H₂O, not NaHCO₃.
Round 5: Attempt 25 mixed GA questions.
F. SSC GK Rule
Never study GK like a story. Study it like a mapping table:
76/77

Thing → Person
Place → State
Article → Feature
Book → Author
Disease → Agent
Vitamin → Deficiency
Dam → River
Dance → State
Award → Field
Scheme → Ministry
That is exactly how SSC usually converts GK into marks.
This completes Part 3 to Part 13.
SSoouurrcceess
77/77

items: Deep Research - Cracking SSC GK/GS
So i am about to crack to SSC Phase 14 and CGL 2026
exam but i need your help in Cracking GK/GS
I’ll build this around SSC-style GK/GS, not generic “general knowledge.” I’ll verify the latest
official pattern first, then give you a practical roadmap with daily targets and scoring
priorities.
High ROI GK//GS Topics —— SSC CGL//CHSL Focus
1.. Polity // Constitution
Preamble
Fundamental Rights
DPSP
Fundamental Duties
Articles
President
Prime Minister
Parliament
Supreme Court // High Court
I checked the current SSC pattern/trend sources. One important correction: I won’t label
any single question as truly “90% guaranteed,” because SSC randomizes shifts; I’ll mark
them as 90+ revision-priority concepts based on repeatability + syllabus fit + multi-exam
overlap.
SSC GK/GS Deep-Dive: CGL / CHSL / Selection Post Phase
Scope: 2021–2026 trend only, with 2026 treated as notification + expected pattern, not
completed PYQ data. CGL 2026 and Selection Post Phase 14 both keep General Awareness
at 25 questions / 50 marks; Selection Post Phase 14 also has 25 GA questions with
negative marking, and its GK syllabus includes current affairs, awards, books, sports,
scientific research, static GK, portfolios, and persons in news.
Oliveboard Testbook
Important correction: no honest mentor can say “this exact question has 90% probability.”
What we can say is: these concepts have 90+ revision priority because they repeatedly fit
SSC’s PYQ style, syllabus, one-liner nature, and cross-exam overlap. SSC CGL/CHSL GA
repeatedly clusters around Science, Polity, History, Current Affairs, Static GK, Geography,
and Economy; CGL sources put current affairs around 4–5 questions, Science 4–5 or higher,
Polity/History around 3–4 each, while CHSL trend also gives high weight to History,
Science, Polity, Static GK, Geography, and Economics.
Oliveboard Testbook Oliveboard
How SSC asks GK/GS
SSC usually asks direct fact-based questions, not UPSC-style analysis: “Which Article?”,
“Who wrote?”, “Which vitamin?”, “Which river?”, “Which founder?”, “Which state?”, “Which
dance?”, “Which disease-agent?”, “Which amendment?” In older CGL guidance, the GA
section is 25 questions and is high-scoring because it is fast if facts are revised; the listed
static clusters include History, Geography, Economy, Polity, Biology, Chemistry, Physics,
Current Affairs, and Miscellaneous GK.
Jagranjosh.com
Your priority order for CGL/CHSL/Phase 14 GK should be:
Science + Polity + History = core marks
Current Affairs + Static GK = swing marks
Geography + Economy = low-volume but easy-return marks

sections.title: Current Affairs — 15 Highest-ROI Question Frames
items: For Current Affairs, don’t memorize old fixed answers blindly. SSC usually asks from the
last 6–8 months, especially appointments, awards, sports, schemes, summits, reports,
defence, and space.
Oliveboard
Prepare these 15 frames every month:
1. Who is the current head/chairperson of major constitutional/statutory bodies?
2. Recently appointed chiefs: Army, Navy, Air Force, CJI, Election Commissioner, RBI,
ISRO.
3. Latest Padma Awards / Bharat Ratna / major national awards.
4. Latest Nobel Prize winners and fields.
5. Latest Booker / Oscar / Grammy / Sahitya Akademi winners.
6. Host country/city of G20, BRICS, SCO, ASEAN, COP summits.
7. India’s rank in major reports: HDI, Gender Gap, Global Hunger Index, Happiness, Press
Freedom.
8. New government schemes: ministry, target group, launch year.
9. Defence exercises: India + partner country + service branch.
10. DRDO missile/tests: missile name + type + range/use.
11. ISRO missions: launch vehicle, satellite, objective, launch site.
12. Sports winners: Cricket, Hockey, Badminton, Chess, Grand Slams,
Olympics/Commonwealth/Asian events.
13. New GI tags: product + state.
14. New Ramsar sites / national parks / tiger reserves / UNESCO sites.
15. Important days and themes of the current year.

sections.title: Your 90+ Revision Strategy
items: For every topic above, revise in this order:
PYQ-style facts → reverse facts → nearby facts → one-line mock test.
Example:
“Article 32 = Constitutional Remedies”
Reverse: “Constitutional Remedies = Article 32”
Nearby: “Article 226 = High Court writ power”
Mock: “Which Article is called the heart and soul of the Constitution?”
Do this for 30 days and your GK/GS will stop feeling random.
One note: I’m using a research-style filtering approach here—multi-source patterning,
syllabus mapping, and repeatability scoring—similar to the uploaded deep-research
concept of synthesizing many sources into a usable report.
Got it. You don’t want 15 per broad subject; you want 10 ultra-high-ROI questions from
every micro-topic you listed, strictly for SSC CGL + SSC Selection Post Phase 14. I’ll
restructure it that way and start with a usable batch instead of compressing it.
Understood. Your exact requirement is:
SSC CGL + SSC Selection Post Phase 14 only
Micro-topic wise
10 high-ROI PYQ-style questions from each micro-topic
Not 15 from broad subjects.

11. Current Affairs — 15 Highest-ROI Question Frames
For Current Affairs, don’t memorize old fixed answers blindly. SSC usually asks from the
last 6–8 months, especially appointments, awards, sports, schemes, summits, reports,
defence, and space.

Prepare these 15 frames every month:
1. Who is the current head/chairperson of major constitutional/statutory bodies?
2. Recently appointed chiefs: Army, Navy, Air Force, CJI, Election Commissioner, RBI,
ISRO.

15. Important days and themes of the current year.
Your 90+ Revision Strategy
For every topic above, revise in this order:
PYQ-style facts → reverse facts → nearby facts → one-line mock test.
Example:
“Article 32 = Constitutional Remedies”
Reverse: “Constitutional Remedies = Article 32”
Nearby: “Article 226 = High Court writ power”
Mock: “Which Article is called the heart and soul of the Constitution?”
Do this for 30 days and your GK/GS will stop feeling random.

sections.title: D. 90+ Priority Zones
items: For SSC CGL + Phase 14, these are your strongest zones:
1. Polity articles and amendments
2. Modern history movements
3. INC sessions
4. Governor-General/Viceroy events
5. Vitamins and diseases
6. Biology organs and hormones
7. Physics SI units and instruments
8. Chemistry formulas and alloys
9. Rivers, dams, lakes, passes
10. National parks and wildlife sanctuaries
11. Books and authors
12. Awards and trophies
13. Dance/festival-state mapping
14. Government schemes
15. Last 6–8 months current affairs

sections.title: E. How to Convert This Into Marks
items: For every micro-topic:
Round 1: Read all 10 Q&A.
Round 2: Hide answer and recall.
Round 3: Reverse the question.
Example: “Baking soda = NaHCO₃” becomes “NaHCO₃ = Baking soda.”
Round 4: Make one trap fact.
Example: Washing soda = Na₂CO₃·10H₂O, not NaHCO₃.
Round 5: Attempt 25 mixed GA questions.

sections.title: F. SSC GK Rule
items: Never study GK like a story. Study it like a mapping table:
Thing → Person
Place → State
Article → Feature
Book → Author
Disease → Agent
Vitamin → Deficiency
Dam → River
Dance → State
Award → Field
Scheme → Ministry
That is exactly how SSC usually converts GK into marks.
This completes Part 3 to Part 13.
